value.market_read: 3-2-1 Crack: The 3-2-1 settled at $49.83/bbl, down $1.21 w/w, as product complexes lagged a sharp move lower in crude. The story this week is WTI itself — front-month settled at $74.14, off $9.21 w/w following the June 12 WPSR's 8.26 mb crude draw and a 1.61 mb Cushing draw, with refinery utilization a robust 96.7%. With crude falling faster than the modest pullback in margin, the crack compressed only marginally despite the violent flat-price reset; the structure remains distillate-led at elevated absolute levels. Watch: Whether downstream prints catch down to crude into next Wednesday's release, or whether the 96.7% run rate keeps product output ample enough to cap further crack expansion. Gasoline Crack: The RBOB crack settled at $47.34/bbl, down $1.85 w/w — the weakest sub-component of the complex this week. A 0.91 mb gasoline draw was supportive on paper, but it came in lighter than the seasonal Memorial-Day-through-July-4 demand pull would typically demand, leaving cracks to soften as RBOB futures underperformed even the falling crude. With runs at 96.7%, the bid for incremental gasoline barrels is muted. Watch: Driving-season pull — a sub-1 mb draw against peak summer demand is a yellow flag for gasoline-led margin into July. Distillate Crack: The ULSD crack settled at $54.82/bbl, the firmest leg of the barrel and the reason the regime reads distillate-led. Distillate stocks built 0.95 mb on the week, a counter-seasonal loosening, yet the crack held up — diesel demand from freight and ongoing tightness in middle distillates globally (post-Russia sanction reroutes) continue to underpin ULSD value relative to crude. With dist exports running 1.51 mb/d, US barrels are clearing into a still-firm Atlantic Basin. Watch: Whether the 0.95 mb build is the start of a trend or a one-week blip; a second consecutive build would pressure the strongest crack on the board. Jet Crack: The jet crack settled at $42.32/bbl, the lowest of the four product cracks, consistent with summer yield economics. With distillate cracks commanding $54.82, refiners are incentivized to lean the regrade toward ULSD over jet, capping jet's relative value even as summer air-travel demand peaks. The absolute level remains healthy, but jet sits structurally behind diesel in the middle-distillate pecking order this week. Watch: Summer travel throughput versus refiner regrade decisions — sustained jet demand could narrow the diesel-over-jet gap and pull yield back. Brent–WTI Spread: Brent–WTI settled at $4.00/bbl, a narrow print that reflects WTI's relative resilience even after a $9.21 w/w flat-price drop. A narrow spread weakens the US export arb — yet the latest EIA data still shows crude exports at 4.33 mb/d, gasoline at 0.99 mb/d, and distillate at 1.51 mb/d, all credible volumes, so flows are clearing rather than shut. To re-widen the arb and restore margin on the marginal export barrel, WTI would need to fall further relative to Brent. Watch: If the spread compresses toward $3, expect retained domestic crude to build and pressure WTI; a re-widening requires WTI weakness, not strength.
value.chart_321: At $49.83 the 3-2-1 sits in the upper portion of its trailing 20-print range (high $54.54, low $43.47) and comfortably above the 5-yr seasonal band typical for mid-June. The recent trajectory — climbing from $43.47 to a $51.04 peak before this week's $1.21 dip — shows a complex that firmed through the front of driving season and is now plateauing. The move is distillate-led rather than gasoline-led, which is unusual for June and signals diesel strength is doing the heavy lifting on margin.
value.chart_gas: The gasoline crack at $47.34, down $1.85 w/w, is the soft spot in an otherwise firm barrel — the post-Memorial-Day driving pull that normally lifts RBOB cracks into July is underwhelming this cycle. The lighter-than-expected 0.91 mb gasoline draw against peak summer demand suggests supply is keeping pace at 96.7% utilization, capping gasoline-led upside. Summer-spec RBOB should still find a floor, but the regime tells you margin leadership has shifted to distillate.
value.chart_dist: The distillate crack at $54.82 is the standout, holding firm despite a counter-seasonal 0.95 mb stock build — a sign diesel demand from trucking/freight and a structurally tight global middle-distillate balance are outweighing the domestic loosening. ULSD strength here is the engine behind the distillate-led regime, atypical for mid-summer when gasoline normally leads. Watch whether freight demand and export pull (1.51 mb/d) keep the crack bid if builds continue.
value.chart_jet: The jet crack at $42.32 is the weakest of the four product legs, sitting structurally behind diesel as the regrade favors ULSD ($54.82) over jet yield. Summer air-travel demand is supportive in absolute terms but isn't enough to close the diesel-over-jet gap, so refiners keep leaning middle-distillate output toward diesel. A sustained travel surge would be needed to pull regrade economics back toward jet.
value.chart_bw: Brent–WTI at $4.00 is a narrow spread that reflects WTI's relative firmness even after a $9.21 w/w flat-price drop — narrow here means a weaker, not stronger, US export arb on the marginal barrel. Despite that, actual export volumes (crude 4.33 mb/d, distillate 1.51 mb/d) remain healthy, so the arb is tighter but not shut. With OPEC+ supply discipline and the June 12 crude draw supporting WTI, a re-widening that restores fuller export economics would require WTI to soften relative to Brent.
